title: Extraction and purification of viruses from stream biofilms
author: Meriem Bekliz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.32qgqdw
materials: Recover the supernatant for downstream analyses.
Pre-warm SDS at 37°C and CTAB at 65°C.

steps.title: Preparations - 200 x TE Buffer
steps.instruction: Preparations - 200 x TE Buffer: 2M Tris-HCl (pH 8.5) + 0.2M EDTA. - 1 x TE buffer: 10 mM Tris + 1 mM EDTA (pH 8.0). - 10 x DNase buffer: 100 mM Tris (pH 7.5) + 5mM CaCl 2 + 25 mM MgCl 2 .

steps.title: Centrifuge biofilm sample at 100 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge biofilm sample at 100 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C. 00:15:00

steps.title: Use TFF to concentrate viruses following instruction posted here: https://www
steps.instruction: Use TFF to concentrate viruses following instruction posted here: https://www.protocols.io/view/Phage-Tangential-Flow-Filtration-c7yzpv/

steps.title: Compare to protocol
steps.instruction: Compare to protocol: https://www.protocols.io/view/SYBR-Green-or-Gold-Staining-c7sznd Because of high background noise in biofilm samples, it is important that VLP are counted after purification and DNase I treatment.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1 volumes of 200 x TE buffer, 0.01 volumes of 0.5M EDTA (pH 8) and 1 volume of formamide to sample. Mix well and incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes. 00:30:00

steps.title: DNase I digestion Add 0
steps.instruction: DNase I digestion Add 0.1 volumes of DNase buffer 10 x.

steps.title: Add 5 mM (final concentration) of...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mM (final concentration) of tetrasodium pyrophosphate to the concentrated biofilm sample.

steps.title: Sucrose gradient ultracentrifugation Compare to protocol posted here: https://www
steps.instruction: Sucrose gradient ultracentrifugation Compare to protocol posted here: https://www.protocols.io/view/Purifying-Viruses-Using-Sucrose-Cushion-c3wypd

steps.title: Incubate in dark for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate in dark for 15 minutes. 00:15:00

steps.title: Sonicate the sample on ice three...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the sample on ice three times for 1 minute with 30 second intervals. Shake samples between sonication intervals.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3234 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3234 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C. 00:15:00

steps.title: Filter sequentially the supernatant through 0
steps.instruction: Filter sequentially the supernatant through 0.8 and 0.45 µm filter, respectively.

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 U/µL (final concentration) of DNase I enzyme.

steps.title: Incubate for 3 hours at 37˚C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 3 hours at 37˚C. 03:00:00

steps.title: Heat-inactivate DNase I enzyme at 65°C for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Heat-inactivate DNase I enzyme at 65°C for 15 minutes. 00:15:00

steps.title: Check for microbial contamination with a...
steps.instruction: Check for microbial contamination with a standard PCR using universal 16S rRNA gene primers and gel electrophoresis. See for instance: https://www.protocols.io/view/16S-PCR-Universal-c5my45

steps.title: Pipette 3 mL of 66% sucrose...
steps.instruction: Pipette 3 mL of 66% sucrose and then 7 mL of 30% sucrose into the clean ultracentrifugation tube. Be careful not to mix the gradient.

steps.title: Mark layers with a permanent marker to facilitate layer recognition
steps.instruction: Mark layers with a permanent marker to facilitate layer recognition.

steps.title: Overlay the sample on top of...
steps.instruction: Overlay the sample on top of the gradient and fill to the top. Be careful not to cause any mixing of the gradient.

steps.title: Ultracentrifuge at 106800 x g for 2 hours at 4°C
steps.instruction: Ultracentrifuge at 106800 x g for 2 hours at 4°C. Use a slow acceleration and no brake for the deceleration. 02:00:00

steps.title: Using an 18 G needle, pierce...
steps.instruction: Using an 18 G needle, pierce carefully the tube between the 66% and 30% sucrose layers and extract approximately 2 mL.

steps.title: Stain sample with 2 µL 1000...
steps.instruction: Stain sample with 2 µL 1000 x SYBR Gold and incubate for 30 minutes in the dark at room temperature. 00:30:00

steps.title: Carefully filter the sample onto 0
steps.instruction: Carefully filter the sample onto 0.02 µm pore size membrane filter (Anodisc).

steps.title: Mount filter on glass slide using a drop of VectaShield
steps.instruction: Mount filter on glass slide using a drop of VectaShield.

steps.title: Screen the filter under fluorescence microscope...
steps.instruction: Screen the filter under fluorescence microscope using blue light excitation (488 nm) and green emission (512 nm).

steps.title: Add 2 volumes of 100% EtOH
steps.instruction: Add 2 volumes of 100% EtOH.

steps.title: Incubate at 4°C for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at 4°C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00

steps.title: Centrifuge at 17000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 17000 x g for 20 minutes at 4°C to pellet the DNA. 00:20:00

steps.title: Resuspend DNA with 567 μL of 1 x TE buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend DNA with 567 μL of 1 x TE buffer.

steps.title: Optional stopping point
steps.instruction: Optional stopping point: store the sample at -20°C for up to one month.

steps.title: Add 30 μL of 10% warm...
steps.instruction: Add 30 μL of 10% warm SDS and 3 μL of proteinase K (20 mg/mL).

steps.title: Mix and incubate for 1 hour at 37°C
steps.instruction: Mix and incubate for 1 hour at 37°C. 01:00:00

steps.title: Add 100 μL of 5M NaCl and mix
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of 5M NaCl and mix.

steps.title: Add 80 μL of warm CTAB solution
steps.instruction: Add 80 μL of warm CTAB solution.

steps.title: Vortex and incubate at 65°C for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Vortex and incubate at 65°C for 10 minutes. 00:10:00

steps.title: Add equal volume of chloroform
steps.instruction: Add equal volume of chloroform.

steps.title: Vortex and centrifuge at 16000 x...
steps.instruction: Vortex and centrifuge at 16000 x g for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add equal volume of 25:24:1 phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol
steps.instruction: Add equal volume of 25:24:1 phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol.

steps.title: Vortex, and centrifuge at 16000 x...
steps.instruction: Vortex, and centrifuge at 16000 x g for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add equal volume of chloroform to supernatant
steps.instruction: Add equal volume of chloroform to supernatant.

steps.title: Vortex, and centrifuge at 16000 x...
steps.instruction: Vortex, and centrifuge at 16000 x g for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer top aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.7 volumes of isopropanol to supernatant.

steps.title: Gently mix and incubate at -20°C overnight to precipitate DNA
steps.instruction: Gently mix and incubate at -20°C overnight to precipitate DNA.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16000 x g for 20 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16000 x g for 20 minutes at 4°C. 00:20:00

steps.title: Wash the DNA pellet twice with 70% cold EtOH
steps.instruction: Wash the DNA pellet twice with 70% cold EtOH.

steps.title: Resuspend DNA with 50 μL of DNase-free H 2 O
steps.instruction: Resuspend DNA with 50 μL of DNase-free H 2 O.

steps.title: Quantify DNA (e
steps.instruction: Quantify DNA (e.g. using Qubit according to manufacturer's instructions).

steps.title: Wash the pellet twice with 70%...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet twice with 70% ice cold EtOH and allow to dry at room temperature.
